_comment: strategy_runs.json — user-maintained mapping of strategy IDs to backtest run directories.
Each top-level key is a strategy_id — a stable identifier you assign when you first
generate a strategy.  The convention is:  <coin>_s<N>_<archetype_short>
For each strategy_id the value is an object with these fields:
symbol      (string)           Exchange ticker this strategy trades.
spec_yaml   (string)           Repo-relative path to the strategy YAML.
base_run    (string | null)    Primary in-sample backtest run directory name
Keys are regime labels; values are run directory names.
Keys are descriptive labels; values are run directory names.
(Created by running setup_stress.py on the base run.)
oos_runs    (array of strings) Out-of-sample run directory names, ordered chronologically.
sweep_run   (string | null)    Parameter-sweep run directory name.  null until generated.
The coin prefix (btc_, eth_, ...) prevents name collisions when the same
strategy archetype is run on multiple symbols.
Each run directory contains config.json + artifacts/ (metrics.csv, equity.csv, etc.).
1. Generate a strategy (Stage 2) → edit spec_yaml to point at its YAML.
2. Run backtests (Stage 3) → fill in base_run, regime_runs, stress_runs, oos_runs.
3. Run parameter sweep (Stage 4) → fill in sweep_run.
4. emit_manifest.py (Task 2.12) reads this file to aggregate a strategy's runs
NOTE: emit_manifest.py will raise a clear error if a named run directory is absent
from disk.  This file only records the expected mapping; it does not validate paths.
